title: BLDP Strategic Market Focus: Bus Segment Dominance and Portfolio Shift
text: Ballard Power Systems strategically shifted focus toward the bus segment in 2024-2025, which emerged as the most mature and stable market for fuel cell technology. In 2024, the company secured 1,600 bus engine orders across 7 OEMs, with bus revenue growth of 51% to $44.2 million, offsetting declines in truck, rail, and marine segments. Major OEM partnerships include New Flyer (500-engine, 50-MW agreement through mid-2026), Solaris Bus & Coach (platform win with orders extending through 2026), and Wrightbus (UK bus manufacturer). Heavy Duty Mobility segment revenue declined 38% overall, with bus growth offsetting losses in truck (extended adoption timelines), rail (slower infrastructure development), and marine (early market stage). The shift reflects pragmatic response to market realities where hydrogen bus infrastructure and customer readiness align better than for heavy trucks or emerging verticals. International bus markets, particularly Europe and UK, demonstrated strongest demand with cities implementing hydrogen bus fleets. The company powers over 1,800 fuel cell buses worldwide with over 200 million operational miles and 99% availability rates. This strategic focus on mature, stable bus segment provides revenue foundation while company develops truck, rail, and marine technologies for eventual deployment.
